findings_details.title: Regression test plan-claim.sh stub is dead code — never invoked
findings_details.body: The regression test writes plan-claim.sh into $STUB_DIR (line 90-94) but never sets RQ_PLAN_CLAIM_BIN, so the real plugins/rq/scripts/plan-claim.sh is used instead. The stub is dead code. The test passes incidentally because plan-claim.sh check-claim --local-only returns 'unclaimed' for any issue not bound to a branch-issue.json file. The stub provides no isolation guarantee: if the real plan-claim.sh behavior changes for --local-only, the regression test will silently break. Fix: set RQ_PLAN_CLAIM_BIN=$STUB_DIR/plan-claim.sh before invoking the subject, consistent with make_full_gh_stub.

findings_details.title: Step 0 uses PLUGIN_DIR without canonical preamble
findings_details.body: Step 0 (line 21) sets SPRS=$PLUGIN_DIR/session-prs.sh but PLUGIN_DIR is never derived in Step 0. The canonical preamble (find . -name run-gates.sh ...) is absent. In a fresh subagent context running only Step 0, PLUGIN_DIR is empty and the assignment becomes SPRS=/session-prs.sh. Fix: add the canonical PLUGIN_DIR preamble to Step 0 before the SPRS assignment.

findings_details.title: Step 99 uses SPRS set in Step 0 — cross-step state reuse
findings_details.body: Step 99 (line 175) references $SPRS which was set in Step 0. In a fresh subagent context, SPRS is empty and the command expands to 'bash  refresh'. Fix: re-derive both PLUGIN_DIR (canonical preamble) and SPRS in Step 99.

findings_details.title: Step 3 uses PLUGIN_DIR without re-deriving it
findings_details.body: Step 3 — Bind (line 102) references $PLUGIN_DIR but the canonical preamble is absent from this step. In a subagent context that enters directly at Step 3, PLUGIN_DIR is empty. Fix: add the canonical PLUGIN_DIR preamble to the Step 3 code block.

findings_details.title: fetch_open_pr_issue_refs silently truncates at 100 PRs
findings_details.body: gh pr list -L 100 silently drops open PRs beyond the 100th. For repos with >100 open PRs, active-work signals on those PRs are never detected. An issue referenced by PR #101+ will appear as a free candidate and be offered for binding, causing concurrent-session collision. Fix: use the gh API pagination or set -L to a larger bound (e.g. 500) with a comment explaining the cap.

findings_details.title: plan-claim.sh stub in make_full_gh_stub is dead code
findings_details.body: The stub writes plan-claim.sh into $stub_dir and puts $stub_dir on PATH, but rq-next-suggest.sh invokes plan-claim.sh via absolute path (sd + '/plan-claim.sh' where sd=SCRIPT_DIR). The PATH-based stub is never called. Tests silently use the real plan-claim.sh with --local-only, which happens to return 'unclaimed' for the test issue numbers. This is a fragile implicit dependency: if a real worktree or branch named issue-999-* or issue-100-* exists in the repo, the test will incorrectly block those candidates. Fix: export SCRIPT_DIR pointing to a temp stub dir that contains the plan-claim.sh stub, or pass an env var the script can use to override the plan-claim.sh path.
